{"step_by_step_thinking": "Long face syndrome is characterized by an increased lower facial height, which results in a vertically elongated facial profile. In such cases, the palatal plane, which is a reference plane that runs through the palate, typically shifts in response to changes in the lower facial height. When the lower facial height increases, the lower face extends downward, causing the palatal plane to tilt posteriorly and downward. This is due to the increased vertical dimension of the lower face, which pulls the palatal plane into a more posterior and inferior orientation. Therefore, the palatal plane will be posteriorly downward in such a patient. This aligns with the anatomical principle that increased lower facial height leads to a posteriorly downward displacement of the palatal plane.", "answer_choice": "A"}
